title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for reader outcomes, repository baseline, static architecture, and evidence and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.
Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Evidence Ladder symptom in reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter during this quiz case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing

stimulus.title: Evidence Ladder failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A visually correct local page proves the published site is complete.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

options.text: Accept the Evidence Ladder result because visible reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Evidence Ladder.

For diagnose the hidden failure, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Evidence Ladder for reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter.
Run the Evidence Ladder boundary case for reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Evidence Ladder: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 5987 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Retrieving Evidence Ladder through the concrete reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.
